focal_character.name: Wanjiku Mwangi
focal_character.occupation: medical records clerk at a public hospital in Nairobi
focal_character.ordinary_life_state: Back at her hospital desk, Wanjiku faces a fused memo with ten requests and three signatures, then rewrites each requirement separately and solves them one by one.

problem_structure.unknown: which of the three clauses in the compound patient-file release condition actually binds
data: files stamped REQUEST PENDING — CONDITION UNMET
the compound release paragraph with three clauses
the featureless central panel on the far wall
the abandoned wrong-approach arrangements: size-sorted piles, categorical grid, and inventory notes
problem_structure.condition: a patient file may be released only if next-of-kin consent, a low balance, and a physician countersignature all hold

gate_details.correct_approach: Wanjiku split the compound condition into individual clauses, tested each one alone by covering the others with her hand, identified the binding clause, and touched the panel; the gate opened.

new_texture: Fused memos stamped URGENT — ACTION REQUIRED land on clerks' desks with multiple supervisor signatures.
A billing colleague across the aisle notices when someone stops treating the whole memo as one problem and goes still.
Office noise dips for a breath when a clerk changes register before anyone has spoken.

anchor_update.observation:
Grade 2. Executor approach. Room organised by size sequence — large, medium, small files grouped, spacing even. Underlined: one file outside sequence. Solver did not look at it. Register 2 at minute 8. Register 3 at minute 10.
System builder. Room mirrored categorical grid — six columns, chronological rows. Perfect assignment. Underlined: condition not in categories. Register flicker 1/3 throughout. No true Register 2.
Information gatherer. Room gave complete inventory — all objects logged, all surfaces noted. Underlined: no question asked of data. Register 1 entire session.
Correct approach: condition read clause by clause. Hand covered extraneous text. Register 3 at minute 52. Gate closed minute 53.
anchor_update.reader_can_suspect_update: The anchor now leaves physical traces inside gates that document multiple wrong approaches and the correct clause-by-clause condition separation in real time.

macro_mystery_update.evidence: A trace inside the gate room records three different solver approaches, the gap each missed, and the exact minute the condition was read clause by clause.
